metadata.name: int_jira__field_history_scd
metadata.comment: Slowly-changing-dimension model that fills values from differnt fields in the pivoted daily history model. Note: this is the singular field history-related model that is not incremental (materialized as table)

valid_starting_on.name: valid_starting_on
valid_starting_on.comment: Calendar date of when the field was changed to this value.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` whose field was updated.

status_id.name: status_id
status_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's `status` (the step that the issue is currently at in the project's workflow).

valid_starting_at_week.name: valid_starting_at_week
valid_starting_at_week.comment: The start date of the week corresponding to the valid_starting_on date.

status.name: status
status.comment: The ID of the status that an issue has on a given day.

sprint.name: sprint
sprint.comment: The ID of the sprint that an issue is slotted into on a given day.

team.name: team
team.comment: The team associated with an issue on a given day (when `jira_using_teams` is enabled).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: int_jira__issue_join
metadata.comment: The core issue table, enhanced with current-status attributes from foreign-key-related tables, and metrics regarding resolutions and assignments.

parent_issue_id.name: parent_issue_id
parent_issue_id.comment: Self-referencing ID of the parent `issue`, which may be an epic.

original_estimate_seconds.name: original_estimate_seconds
original_estimate_seconds.comment: The original estimate of how long working on this issue would take, in seconds.

remaining_estimate_seconds.name: remaining_estimate_seconds
remaining_estimate_seconds.comment: The estimate of how much longer working on this issue will take, in seconds.

time_spent_seconds.name: time_spent_seconds
time_spent_seconds.comment: The time that was spent working on this issue, in seconds.

assignee_user_id.name: assignee_user_id
assignee_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` currently assigned to this task.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was created (in UTC).

resolved_at.name: resolved_at
resolved_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was resolved (ie completed, marked as duplicate). If an issue is un-resolved, this will be null.

creator_user_id.name: creator_user_id
creator_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the `user` who first created the issue. Cannot be changed.

due_date.name: due_date
due_date.comment: Calendar day on which the issue is due, if a due date is provided.

environment.name: environment
environment.comment: Text field describing the environment in which the issue occurred (ie "IE9 on Windows 7").

issue_type_id.name: issue_type_id
issue_type_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue_type`.

priority_id.name: priority_id
priority_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's current `priority`.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the `project` that the issue belongs to.

reporter_user_id.name: reporter_user_id
reporter_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who reported the issue. This differs from the `issue.creator` column  in that the reporter can be changed in-app, though it is initialized as the creator.

resolution_id.name: resolution_id
resolution_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's type of `resolution`.

status_id.name: status_id
status_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's `status` (the step that the issue is currently at  in the project's workflow).

status_changed_at.name: status_changed_at
status_changed_at.comment: Timestamp of when the status was last changed.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was last updated in some way.

work_ratio.name: work_ratio
work_ratio.comment: The percentage of work that has been logged against the issue (time_spent) vs the original estimate of worktime. Equals -1.0 when the fields required for calculation are not provided.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran last synced this record.

issue_type.name: issue_type
issue_type.comment: Name of the issue type (ie Epic, Task, Subtask, any custom types).

parent_issue_type.name: parent_issue_type
parent_issue_type.comment: Name of the type of issue that the parent is.

parent_issue_key.name: parent_issue_key
parent_issue_key.comment: Issue key of the sub-issue's parent.

is_parent_epic.name: is_parent_epic
is_parent_epic.comment: Boolean that is true if the parent is an epic.

assignee_name.name: assignee_name
assignee_name.comment: User display name of the person currently assigned this issue.

assignee_timezone.name: assignee_timezone
assignee_timezone.comment: Timezone of the user currently assigned this issue.

assignee_email.name: assignee_email
assignee_email.comment: Email associated with the user currently assigned this issue.

reporter_email.name: reporter_email
reporter_email.comment: Email associated with the user who reported this issue.

reporter_name.name: reporter_name
reporter_name.comment: User display name of the person who reported the issue.

reporter_timezone.name: reporter_timezone
reporter_timezone.comment: Timezone of the user currently who reported this issue.

current_status.name: current_status
current_status.comment: Title of the category of status.

current_status_category.name: current_status_category
current_status_category.comment: Title of the umbrella status category that the issue is currently in.

resolution_type.name: resolution_type
resolution_type.comment: Display name of the type of resolution that closed the issue.

current_priority.name: current_priority
current_priority.comment: Name of the issue's current priority as it appears in the UI.

current_sprint_id.name: current_sprint_id
current_sprint_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the `sprint` that the issue is currently in.

count_sprint_changes.name: count_sprint_changes
count_sprint_changes.comment: The total number of sprints that the issue has belonged to.

sprint_started_at.name: sprint_started_at
sprint_started_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint began.

sprint_ended_at.name: sprint_ended_at
sprint_ended_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint is planned to end.

sprint_completed_at.name: sprint_completed_at
sprint_completed_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint was completed.

is_active_sprint.name: is_active_sprint
is_active_sprint.comment: Yes, if the sprint started after the current time and if the sprint ends in the future.

first_assigned_at.name: first_assigned_at
first_assigned_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was first assigned to a user.

last_assigned_at.name: last_assigned_at
last_assigned_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was last assigned to a user.

first_resolved_at.name: first_resolved_at
first_resolved_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was first resolved.

fixes_versions.name: fixes_versions
fixes_versions.comment: Comma-separated list of the names of project versions that this issue fixes.

affects_versions.name: affects_versions
affects_versions.comment: Comma-separated list of the names of project versions that this issue affects.

conversation.name: conversation
conversation.comment: Line-separated list of comments made on this issue, including the timestamp and author name of each comment.

count_comments.name: count_comments
count_comments.comment: The number of comments made on this issues.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: int_jira__issue_type_parents
metadata.comment: Table relating issues with data regarding their parent issues (which may be epics). Contains all columns present in `stg_jira__issue`.

original_estimate_seconds.name: original_estimate_seconds
original_estimate_seconds.comment: The original estimate of how long working on this issue would take, in seconds.

remaining_estimate_seconds.name: remaining_estimate_seconds
remaining_estimate_seconds.comment: The estimate of how much longer working on this issue will take, in seconds.

time_spent_seconds.name: time_spent_seconds
time_spent_seconds.comment: The time that was spent working on this issue, in seconds.

assignee_user_id.name: assignee_user_id
assignee_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` currently assigned to this task.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was created (in UTC).

resolved_at.name: resolved_at
resolved_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was resolved (ie completed, marked as duplicate). If an issue is un-resolved, this will be null.

creator_user_id.name: creator_user_id
creator_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the `user` who first created the issue. Cannot be changed.

due_date.name: due_date
due_date.comment: Calendar day on which the issue is due, if a due date is provided.

environment.name: environment
environment.comment: Text field describing the environment in which the issue occurred (ie "IE9 on Windows 7").

issue_type_id.name: issue_type_id
issue_type_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue_type`.

parent_issue_id.name: parent_issue_id
parent_issue_id.comment: Unique ID of the parent issue.

priority_id.name: priority_id
priority_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's current `priority`.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `project` that the issue belongs to.

reporter_user_id.name: reporter_user_id
reporter_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who reported the issue. This differs from the `creator` column  in that the reporter can be changed in-app.

resolution_id.name: resolution_id
resolution_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's type of `resolution`.

status_id.name: status_id
status_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's `status` (the step that the issue is currently at  in the project's workflow).

status_changed_at.name: status_changed_at
status_changed_at.comment: Timestamp of when the status was last changed.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was last updated in some way.

work_ratio.name: work_ratio
work_ratio.comment: The percentage of work that has been logged against the issue (time_spent) vs the original estimate of worktime. Equals -1.0 when the fields required for calculation are not provided.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran last synced this record.

revised_parent_issue_id.name: revised_parent_issue_id
revised_parent_issue_id.comment: Unique ID of the parent issue, coalesced with the ID of any linked epics (in classic projects).

issue_type.name: issue_type
issue_type.comment: Name of the issue type (ie Epic, Task, Subtask, any custom types).

parent_issue_type.name: parent_issue_type
parent_issue_type.comment: Name of the parent issue type (ie Epic, Task, Subtask, any custom types).

parent_issue_key.name: parent_issue_key
parent_issue_key.comment: UI-facing id of the parent issue.

is_parent_epic.name: is_parent_epic
is_parent_epic.comment: Boolean that is true if the parent is an epic.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: int_jira__pivot_daily_field_history
metadata.comment: Table pivoting out the fields in `int_jira__daily_field_history` into columns, in which the value of each field column is the latest value updated a given day.

valid_starting_on.name: valid_starting_on
valid_starting_on.comment: Calendar date of when the field was changed to this value.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` whose field was updated.

valid_starting_at_week.name: valid_starting_at_week
valid_starting_at_week.comment: The start date of the week corresponding to the valid_starting_on date.

status.name: status
status.comment: The ID of the status that an issue has on a given day.

sprint.name: sprint
sprint.comment: The ID of the sprint that an issue is slotted into on a given day.

team.name: team
team.comment: The team associated with an issue on a given day (when `jira_using_teams` is enabled).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__daily_issue_field_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a daily snapshot of an issue's field values (status and sprint by default, plus any fields added to the `issue_field_history_columns` variable). Each issue will have a record for every day in which it was open and/or being updated. For currently open issues, the latest date will be the current date, and for closed issues, this will be the last time the issue was resolved OR updated in any way (plus a configurable buffer afterward, set to a month by default). Note: this is an incremental model.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: Date on which the issue had these field values.

date_week.name: date_week
date_week.comment: The start date of the week corresponding to `date_day`.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` with these values.

status.name: status
status.comment: The name of the status that an issue has on a given day.

status_id.name: status_id
status_id.comment: The ID of the status that an issue has on a given day.

sprint.name: sprint
sprint.comment: The ID of the sprint that an issue is slotted into on a given day.

sprint_name.name: sprint_name
sprint_name.comment: The name of the sprint that an issue is slotted into on a given day. Returns null when `jira_using_sprints` is disabled.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__daily_sprint_issue_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a daily snapshot of a sprint and each of its assigned issues at the time. Sprints are tracked from their start date to their end date (or current date if no end date is provided).

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` with these sprint values.

date_day.name: date_day
date_day.comment: Date on which the sprint and issue had these field values.

date_week.name: date_week
date_week.comment: The start date of the week corresponding to `date_day`.

status.name: status
status.comment: The name of the status that an issue has on a given day.

sprint_id.name: sprint_id
sprint_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `sprint` with these values.

sprint_started_at.name: sprint_started_at
sprint_started_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint began.

sprint_ended_at.name: sprint_ended_at
sprint_ended_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint is planned to end.

sprint_completed_at.name: sprint_completed_at
sprint_completed_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint was completed.

board_id.name: board_id
board_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `board` that the sprint belongs to.

issue_created_at.name: issue_created_at
issue_created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was created (in UTC).

issue_resolved_at.name: issue_resolved_at
issue_resolved_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was resolved (in UTC).

assignee_user_id.name: assignee_user_id
assignee_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` currently assigned to this task.

assignee_name.name: assignee_name
assignee_name.comment: User display name of the person currently assigned this issue.

original_estimate_seconds.name: original_estimate_seconds
original_estimate_seconds.comment: The original estimate of how long working on this issue would take, in seconds.

remaining_estimate_seconds.name: remaining_estimate_seconds
remaining_estimate_seconds.comment: The estimate of how much longer working on this issue will take, in seconds.

time_spent_seconds.name: time_spent_seconds
time_spent_seconds.comment: The time that was spent working on this issue, in seconds.

is_sprint_active.name: is_sprint_active
is_sprint_active.comment: Boolean indicating if the sprint is currently active (started but not completed).

is_sprint_completed.name: is_sprint_completed
is_sprint_completed.comment: Boolean indicating if the sprint has been completed.

is_issue_open.name: is_issue_open
is_issue_open.comment: Boolean indicating if the issue is currently open (not resolved).

is_issue_resolved_in_sprint.name: is_issue_resolved_in_sprint
is_issue_resolved_in_sprint.comment: Boolean indicating if the issue was resolved during this sprint.

days_left_in_sprint.name: days_left_in_sprint
days_left_in_sprint.comment: Number of days remaining in the sprint from the given date (null if sprint is completed or date is after sprint end).

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

remaining_estimate_seconds.name: remaining_estimate_seconds
remaining_estimate_seconds.comment: The estimate of how much longer working on this issue will take, in seconds.

time_spent_seconds.name: time_spent_seconds

priority_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's current `priority`.

reporter_user_id.name: reporter_user_id
reporter_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who reported the issue. This differs from the `issue.creator` column  in that the reporter can be changed in-app, though it is initialized as the creator.

resolution_id.name: resolution_id
resolution_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's type of `resolution`.

status_id.name: status_id

updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was last updated in some way.

work_ratio.name: work_ratio

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran last synced this record.

issue_type.name: issue_type
issue_type.comment: Name of the issue type (ie Epic, Task, Subtask, any custom types).

parent_issue_type.name: parent_issue_type
parent_issue_type.comment: Name of the type of issue that the parent is.

parent_issue_key.name: parent_issue_key
parent_issue_key.comment: Issue key of the sub-issue's parent/epic.

is_parent_epic.name: is_parent_epic
is_parent_epic.comment: Boolean that is true if the parent is an epic.

assignee_name.name: assignee_name
assignee_name.comment: User display name of the person currently assigned this issue.

assignee_timezone.name: assignee_timezone
assignee_timezone.comment: Timezone of the user currently assigned this issue.

assignee_email.name: assignee_email
assignee_email.comment: Email associated with the user currently assigned this issue.

reporter_email.name: reporter_email
reporter_email.comment: Email associated with the user who reported this issue.

reporter_name.name: reporter_name
reporter_name.comment: User display name of the person who reported the issue.

reporter_timezone.name: reporter_timezone
reporter_timezone.comment: Timezone of the user currently who reported this issue.

current_status.name: current_status
current_status.comment: Title of the category of status.

current_status_category.name: current_status_category
current_status_category.comment: Title of the umbrella status category that the issue is currently in.

resolution_type.name: resolution_type
resolution_type.comment: Display name of the type of resolution that closed the issue.

current_priority.name: current_priority
current_priority.comment: Name of the issue's current priority as it appears in the UI.

current_sprint_id.name: current_sprint_id
current_sprint_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the current `sprint` associate with the issue.

current_sprint_name.name: current_sprint_name
current_sprint_name.comment: Title of the current sprint associated with the issue.

count_sprint_changes.name: count_sprint_changes
count_sprint_changes.comment: The total number of sprints that the issue has belonged to.

sprint_started_at.name: sprint_started_at
sprint_started_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint began.

sprint_ended_at.name: sprint_ended_at

sprint_completed_at.name: sprint_completed_at

is_active_sprint.name: is_active_sprint
is_active_sprint.comment: Yes, if the sprint started after the current time and if the sprint ends in the future.

first_assigned_at.name: first_assigned_at
first_assigned_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was first assigned to a user.

last_assigned_at.name: last_assigned_at
last_assigned_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was last assigned to a user.

first_resolved_at.name: first_resolved_at
first_resolved_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was first resolved.

fixes_versions.name: fixes_versions
fixes_versions.comment: Comma-separated list of the names of project versions that this issue fixes.

affects_versions.name: affects_versions
affects_versions.comment: Comma-separated list of the names of project versions that this issue affects.

conversation.name: conversation
conversation.comment: Line-separated list of comments made on this issue, including the timestamp and author name of each comment. (Disabled by default for Redshift.)

count_comments.name: count_comments
count_comments.comment: The number of comments made on this issues.

open_duration_seconds.name: open_duration_seconds
open_duration_seconds.comment: The number of seconds that the issue has spent open (until resolution, or the current timestamp).

any_assignment_duration_seconds.name: any_assignment_duration_seconds
any_assignment_duration_seconds.comment: The number of seconds that the issue has spent assigned (until resolution, or the current timestamp).

last_assignment_duration_seconds.name: last_assignment_duration_seconds
last_assignment_duration_seconds.comment: The number of seconds that the issue has spent assigned to the current/most recent user (until resolution, or the current timestamp).

status.name: status
status.comment: The name of the status the issue most recently had.

sprint_name.name: sprint_name
sprint_name.comment: List of associated sprint names. Returns null when `jira_using_sprints` is disabled.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__issue_status_transitions
metadata.comment: Each record represents a status transition for an issue, tracking the complete workflow journey with timing metrics and transition analysis. The grain is issue_id + status_sequence, providing a chronological view of how issues move through different statuses over time.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue.

status.name: status
status.comment: The status name during this transition period.

status_category_name.name: status_category_name
status_category_name.comment: The category of the status (To Do, In Progress, Done).

previous_status.name: previous_status
previous_status.comment: The status the issue transitioned from (null for initial status).

previous_status_category_name.name: previous_status_category_name
previous_status_category_name.comment: The category of the previous status.

transition_at.name: transition_at
transition_at.comment: Timestamp when the issue transitioned to this status.

transition_until.name: transition_until
transition_until.comment: Timestamp when the issue transitioned out of this status (or current time if still in status).

status_sequence.name: status_sequence
status_sequence.comment: Sequential number indicating the order of status transitions (1 = first, incrementing chronologically).

is_current_status.name: is_current_status
is_current_status.comment: Boolean indicating if this is the current active status for the issue.

minutes_in_status.name: minutes_in_status
minutes_in_status.comment: Time spent in this status, measured in minutes.

minutes_in_previous_status.name: minutes_in_previous_status
minutes_in_previous_status.comment: Time spent in the previous status before this transition, measured in minutes (null for initial status).

transition_direction.name: transition_direction
transition_direction.comment: Categorizes the workflow direction: 'new' (initial), 'forward' (toward completion), 'backward' (reopening/regression), 'lateral' (within same category), or 'other'.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__project_enhanced
metadata.comment: Table of projects enriched with data about its lead, epics, components, and volume and velocity of worktime.

project_description.name: project_description
project_description.comment: Description of the project, if given.

project_key.name: project_key
project_key.comment: UI-facing ID of the project. This becomes the default prefix for tasks created within this project.

project_lead_user_id.name: project_lead_user_id
project_lead_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who leads this project.

project_category_id.name: project_category_id
project_category_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `project_category` that the project is associated with, if any.

permission_scheme_id.name: permission_scheme_id
permission_scheme_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `permission_scheme` that the project ascribes to.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran last synced this record.

project_lead_user_name.name: project_lead_user_name
project_lead_user_name.comment: User display name of the person leading this project.

project_lead_email.name: project_lead_email
project_lead_email.comment: Email associated with the project lead.

epics.name: epics
epics.comment: Comma-separated list of epics worked on in this project.

components.name: components
components.comment: Comma-separated list of components of this project.

count_closed_issues.name: count_closed_issues
count_closed_issues.comment: The number of issues closed in this project.

count_open_issues.name: count_open_issues
count_open_issues.comment: The number of currently un-resolved issues in the project.

count_open_assigned_issues.name: count_open_assigned_issues
count_open_assigned_issues.comment: The number of currently open and currently assigned issues in the project.

avg_close_time_days.name: avg_close_time_days
avg_close_time_days.comment: The average time taken to close issues, in days.

avg_assigned_close_time_days.name: avg_assigned_close_time_days
avg_assigned_close_time_days.comment: The average time taken to close issues from their first assignment, in days.

avg_age_currently_open_days.name: avg_age_currently_open_days
avg_age_currently_open_days.comment: The average age of any currently-open issues.

avg_age_currently_open_assigned_days.name: avg_age_currently_open_assigned_days
avg_age_currently_open_assigned_days.comment: The average age of any currently-open issues, from their first assignment.

median_close_time_days.name: median_close_time_days
median_close_time_days.comment: The median time taken to close issues, in days.

median_age_currently_open_days.name: median_age_currently_open_days
median_age_currently_open_days.comment: The median age of any currently-open issues.

median_assigned_close_time_days.name: median_assigned_close_time_days
median_assigned_close_time_days.comment: The median time taken to close issues from their first assignment, in days.

median_age_currently_open_assigned_days.name: median_age_currently_open_assigned_days
median_age_currently_open_assigned_days.comment: The median age of any currently-open issues, from their first assignment.

avg_close_time_seconds.name: avg_close_time_seconds
avg_close_time_seconds.comment: The average time taken to close issues, in seconds.

avg_assigned_close_time_seconds.name: avg_assigned_close_time_seconds
avg_assigned_close_time_seconds.comment: The average time taken to close issues from their first assignment, in seconds.

avg_age_currently_open_seconds.name: avg_age_currently_open_seconds
avg_age_currently_open_seconds.comment: The average age of any currently-open issues.

avg_age_currently_open_assigned_seconds.name: avg_age_currently_open_assigned_seconds
avg_age_currently_open_assigned_seconds.comment: The average age of any currently-open issues, from their first assignment.

median_close_time_seconds.name: median_close_time_seconds
median_close_time_seconds.comment: The median time taken to close issues, in seconds.

median_age_currently_open_seconds.name: median_age_currently_open_seconds
median_age_currently_open_seconds.comment: The median age of any currently-open issues.

median_assigned_close_time_seconds.name: median_assigned_close_time_seconds
median_assigned_close_time_seconds.comment: The median time taken to close issues from their first assignment, in seconds.

median_age_currently_open_assigned_secon.name: median_age_currently_open_assigned_seconds
median_age_currently_open_assigned_secon.comment: The median age of any currently-open issues, from their first assignment.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__sprint_enhanced
metadata.comment: This model provides enhanced sprint-level metrics in Jira, incorporating data from sprints, sprint-related issue history, and sprint-based story points tracking.

sprint_started_at.name: sprint_started_at
sprint_started_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint began.

sprint_ended_at.name: sprint_ended_at
sprint_ended_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint is planned to end.

sprint_completed_at.name: sprint_completed_at
sprint_completed_at.comment: Timestamp of when the sprint was completed.

board_id.name: board_id
board_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `board` that the sprint belongs to.

issues_committed.name: issues_committed
issues_committed.comment: Issues committed at the start of a sprint.

open_sprint_issues.name: open_sprint_issues
open_sprint_issues.comment: Issues that were open in a sprint.

resolved_sprint_issues.name: resolved_sprint_issues
resolved_sprint_issues.comment: Issues that were resolved in a sprint.

original_estimate_seconds.name: original_estimate_seconds
original_estimate_seconds.comment: The original estimate of how long working on the issues in this sprint would take, in seconds.

remaining_estimate_seconds.name: remaining_estimate_seconds
remaining_estimate_seconds.comment: The remaining estimate of how long working on the issues in this sprint would take, in seconds.

time_spent_seconds.name: time_spent_seconds
time_spent_seconds.comment: The time that was spent working on the issues in the sprint in seconds.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__timestamp_issue_field_history
metadata.comment: Each record represents a time period when an issue had specific field values. This is an SCD Type 2 table that tracks field changes at the timestamp level, showing the complete field state during each validity period. Each record contains valid_from/valid_until timestamps and an is_current_record flag.

valid_from.name: valid_from
valid_from.comment: Timestamp when this field state became effective.

valid_until.name: valid_until
valid_until.comment: Timestamp when this field state ended (next change or current time).

updated_at_week.name: updated_at_week
updated_at_week.comment: The start date of the week corresponding to the update timestamp.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` with these values.

status_id.name: status_id
status_id.comment: The ID of the status that an issue has during this period.

status.name: status
status.comment: The name of the status that an issue has during this period.

status_category_name.name: status_category_name
status_category_name.comment: The category of the status (To Do, In Progress, Done).

author_id.name: author_id
author_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who made the field changes.

sprint.name: sprint
sprint.comment: The ID of the sprint that an issue is slotted into during this period.

sprint_name.name: sprint_name
sprint_name.comment: The name of the sprint that an issue is slotted into during this period. (Returns no data when `jira_using_sprints` is disabled.)

is_current_record.name: is_current_record
is_current_record.comment: Boolean indicating if this is the current/active field state.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: jira__user_enhanced
metadata.comment: Table of users enriched with their projects, and the volume and velocity of their issues.

email.name: email
email.comment: Email associated with the user acccount.

locale.name: locale
locale.comment: The Java locale of the user.

user_display_name.name: user_display_name
user_display_name.comment: Name of the user as it appears in the UI.

time_zone.name: time_zone
time_zone.comment: The user's timezone, as defined in their settings.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.comment: Boolean showing whether this user is currently active.

_fivetran_synced.name: _fivetran_synced
_fivetran_synced.comment: Timestamp of when Fivetran last synced this record.

projects.name: projects
projects.comment: Comma-separated list of projects on which the user has been assigned to.

count_closed_issues.name: count_closed_issues
count_closed_issues.comment: The number of issues that the user has closed (while being assigned to).

count_open_issues.name: count_open_issues
count_open_issues.comment: The number of currently open issues that the user is assigned to.

avg_close_time_days.name: avg_close_time_days
avg_close_time_days.comment: The average time taken to close issues since assignment, in days.

avg_age_currently_open_days.name: avg_age_currently_open_days
avg_age_currently_open_days.comment: The average age of a user's currently open issues.

median_close_time_days.name: median_close_time_days
median_close_time_days.comment: The median time taken to close issues since assignment, in days.

median_age_currently_open_days.name: median_age_currently_open_days
median_age_currently_open_days.comment: The median age of a user's currently open issues.

avg_close_time_seconds.name: avg_close_time_seconds
avg_close_time_seconds.comment: The average time taken to close issues since assignment, in seconds.

avg_age_currently_open_seconds.name: avg_age_currently_open_seconds
avg_age_currently_open_seconds.comment: The average age of a user's currently open issues.

median_close_time_seconds.name: median_close_time_seconds
median_close_time_seconds.comment: The median time taken to close issues since assignment, in seconds.

median_age_currently_open_seconds.name: median_age_currently_open_seconds
median_age_currently_open_seconds.comment: The median age of a user's currently open issues.

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_jira__comment
metadata.comment: Table of comments made on issues.

author_user_id.name: author_user_id
author_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the `user` id of the comment's author.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the comment was created. TODO - get timezone clarification

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the id of the `issue` that was commented on.

is_public.name: is_public
is_public.comment: Boolean that is true if the comment is visible to all users.

last_update_user_id.name: last_update_user_id
last_update_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the id of the `user` who last updated this comment.

last_updated_at.name: last_updated_at
last_updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the comment was last updated.

metadata.name: stg_jira__field
metadata.comment: Table of all issue fields.

field_id.name: field_id
field_id.comment: Unique ID of the field. Default fields will have descriptive IDs, whereas custom field IDs will be `'customfield_#####'`.

is_array.name: is_array
is_array.comment: Boolean that is true if a field can have multiple values (is multiselect).

is_custom.name: is_custom
is_custom.comment: Boolean that is true if the field is custom to this organization, and false if  it is default to Jira.

field_name.name: field_name
field_name.comment: Name of the field as it appears on issue cards.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_jira__field_option
metadata.comment: Table of all options related to custom fields.

field_id.name: field_id
field_id.comment: The ID of the custom field.

parent_field_id.name: parent_field_id
parent_field_id.comment: The ID of the parent custom field.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_jira__issue
metadata.comment: Table of all issues in your organization's Jira (captures soft deletes).

original_estimate_seconds.name: original_estimate_seconds
original_estimate_seconds.comment: The original estimate of how long working on this issue would take, in seconds.

remaining_estimate_seconds.name: remaining_estimate_seconds
remaining_estimate_seconds.comment: The estimate of how much longer working on this issue will take, in seconds.

time_spent_seconds.name: time_spent_seconds
time_spent_seconds.comment: The time that was spent working on this issue, in seconds.

assignee_user_id.name: assignee_user_id
assignee_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` currently assigned to this task.

created_at.name: created_at
created_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was created (in UTC).

resolved_at.name: resolved_at
resolved_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was resolved (ie completed, marked as duplicate). If an issue is un-resolved, this will be null.

creator_user_id.name: creator_user_id
creator_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the `user` who first created the issue. Cannot be changed.

due_date.name: due_date
due_date.comment: Calendar day on which the issue is due, if a due date is provided.

environment.name: environment
environment.comment: Text field describing the environment in which the issue occurred (ie "IE9 on Windows 7").

issue_type_id.name: issue_type_id
issue_type_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue_type`. This value is generated by coalescing the `work_type` and `issue_type` columns from the source.

priority_id.name: priority_id
priority_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's current `priority`.

project_id.name: project_id
project_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `project` that the issue belongs to.

reporter_user_id.name: reporter_user_id
reporter_user_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who reported the issue. This differs from the `creator` column  in that the reporter can be changed in-app.

resolution_id.name: resolution_id
resolution_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's type of `resolution`.

status_id.name: status_id
status_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the issue's `status` (the step that the issue is currently at  in the project's workflow).

status_changed_at.name: status_changed_at
status_changed_at.comment: Timestamp of when the status was last changed.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue was last updated in some way.

work_ratio.name: work_ratio
work_ratio.comment: The percentage of work that has been logged against the issue (time_spent) vs the original estimate of worktime. Equals -1.0 when the fields required for calculation are not provided.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_jira__issue_field_history
metadata.comment: Table of every value that each **custom non-array** (not multiselect) field has been set to.

field_id.name: field_id
field_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `field` that was changed.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` whose field was updated.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue field was set to this value.

field_value.name: field_value
field_value.comment: Content of the value of that the field was set to.

author_id.name: author_id
author_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who made this field change.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.comment: Boolean showing whether this issue field is currently active.

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

has_stats.label: Has Stats?
has_stats.description: Indicates whether there are statistics for this table

metadata.name: stg_jira__issue_link
metadata.comment: Table of relationships (links) created between issues. Issue links can include blockers, clones/duplicates, and general relationships.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` that is the subject of this relationship (the linker).

related_issue_id.name: related_issue_id
related_issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` that is the object of this relationship (the linkee).

relationship.name: relationship
relationship.comment: The nature of the link between the two issues ("blocks", "is duplicated by", "relates to", etc.)

_fivetran_id.name: _fivetran_id
_fivetran_id.comment: Fivetran-generated ID hashed on field, issue, and value.

field_id.name: field_id
field_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `field` that was changed.

issue_id.name: issue_id
issue_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `issue` whose field was updated.

updated_at.name: updated_at
updated_at.comment: Timestamp of when the issue field was updated to included this value.

field_value.name: field_value
field_value.comment: Content of the value of that the field was set to.

author_id.name: author_id
author_id.comment: Foreign key referencing the ID of the `user` who made this field change.

is_active.name: is_active
is_active.comment: Boolean showing whether this issue field is currently active.

issue_type_id.comment: Unique ID of the issue type.

issue_type_name.name: issue_type_name
issue_type_name.comment: Name of the issue type (ie Epic, Task, Subtask, any custom types)

is_subtask.name: is_subtask
is_subtask.comment: Boolean that is true if this type of issue is a subtask.
